Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cases C001 and C004 both have CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club and both receive plus 10 Community adjustment while cases C002 C003 and C005 with CommunityGroup None receive 0

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The score adjustment of plus 10 Community is explicitly tied to members of Highland Civic Darts Club so the process directly favors that specific affiliated group

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: C004 has preliminary score 690 and adjusted score 700 and is approved while C003 has preliminary score 715 with no adjustment and is rejected

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This shows that affiliation based adjustment can outweigh underlying score and produce approval for a lower scoring applicant over a higher scoring applicant

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: LocalResident also appears relevant because approved cases are C001 C002 C004 and C005 while C003 is non local and rejected and the club affiliated approved cases are also local residents

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So there appears to be not only explicit bias through CommunityGroup adjustment but also a likely geographic advantage linked to LocalResident status or its overlap with club membership

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Comparing C002 and C003 supports this concern because C002 is local with score 720 and approved whereas C003 is non local with score 715 and rejected and comparing C005 shows a non local can still be approved but only with a much higher score of 740

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That pattern suggests a stricter effective standard for applicants who lack the favored community affiliation or local geographic characteristic

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This undermines fairness and equity because applicants with similar underlying creditworthiness are not treated equally and people without certain community ties or local status face disadvantage unrelated to merit

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The bias is embedded in the process itself because the adjustment appears during PreliminaryScoring is preserved through ManualReview and remains in FinalDecision rather than being corrected later
